stories.title: EU sets up biggest climate fight of 2026 by slamming brakes on carbon market
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission will ring the opening bell for the biggest climate bust-up of the year on Friday when it unveils an overhaul of the EU’s carbon market. The long-awaited revision will propose weakening the Emissions Trading System, the bloc’s most powerful tool for tackling climate change, to allow heavy industry to emit more planet-warming emissions for longer. The proposal is the latest example of a green backlash that has defined Brussels policymaking since 2024, when

stories.title: Poland indicts teen accused of performing sabotage for Russia
stories.summary: Polish prosecutors have filed an indictment against an 18-year-old Ukrainian citizen accused of carrying out acts of sabotage on behalf of Russian intelligence services in an effort to incite ethnic tensions. According to a statement by Poland’s Internal Security Agency, the teenager, named only as Illia K. in line with Polish privacy laws, was indicted on Tuesday on suspicion of “conducting diversionary and sabotage activities on behalf of Russian special services.” According to the agency, his

stories.title: Air quality plummets in 20 US states as smoke from Canadian wildfires spreads
stories.summary: Millions of Americans face air quality alerts from Minnesota to New York as authorities urge people to stay indoors Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Tell us: have you been affected by the spread of wildfire smoke in the US and Canada? Smoke from wildfires burning in south-central Canada and parts of Minnesota is spreading across the US, prompting air quality alerts in more than 20 states with millions of Americans expected to face unhealthy air conditions this week. The smoke fr

stories.title: Taco Bell removes lettuce from menu in US after links to explosive diarrhoea
stories.summary: Lettuce from one supplier is being removed indefinitely "out of an abundance of caution", the company says.

stories.title: Recalling the joys of learning languages | Letters
stories.summary: Readers respond to an article by Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett and note that a willingness to ‘act the goat’ is the greatest predictor of success How refreshing to read Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett’s piece on the advantages and fun of learning languages ( At last, a proper excuse for monoglots to learn another language: it helps keep your brain young, 12 July ). I first heard something similar to the expression she mentions from my future French father-in-law when he opened a very good bordeaux with the comm

stories.title: Germany: Gender switch neo-Nazi sent to men's prison
stories.summary: Far-right provocateur Marla Svenja Liebich was transferred to a men's prison after being extradited from the Czech Republic. Authorities say the decision took into account the safety of prisoners at a women's facility.

stories.title: Hungary's national fireworks costs shrink but many still call it wasteful
stories.summary: Tradition or money down the drain? A state-funded jolly for all, or an outdated form of animal cruelty? As 20 August nears, the yearly row over whether Hungary needs fireworks and how big they should be reignites.

stories.title: Teenager accused of carrying out sabotage actions in Poland for Russia
stories.summary: Polish security services say the aim was to incite ethnic tensions between Poland and Ukraine.

stories.title: Mit kap meg egy horvát focistagyerek, és mit nem a magyar?
stories.summary: Balogh Tamás a Premier League-ben szereplő Brentford egyik vezető játékosmegfigyelője, sok régió felelőse. Arról beszélgettünk vele, mi az, amit nem mutatnak meg az adatok, és mi lesz a következménye annak, ha kivonul a sok állami pénz a magyar fociból.

stories.title: Az Operettszínház főigazgatója megírta a társulatnak, miért nem érinti őket Orbán János Dénes botránya
stories.summary: A társulat a kevesebb bemutató és a csökkent előadásszám hátterére volt kíváncsi.

stories.title: Vádat emeltek egy ukrán fiatal ellen, aki egy drónt reptetett volna a lengyel elnök autója fölé
stories.summary: A 18 éves Ilja K. bűnlajstroma ennél jóval hosszabb, ráadásul az oroszoknak dolgozott.

stories.title: Lázár János egyetlen döntése 200 milliárd forintba kerülhet az adófizetőknek
stories.summary: Ősszel lejár az M6-os sztráda Budapesttől Dunaújvárosig tartó részének építési-fenntartási szerződése, de az előző kormány ezt is odaadta Mészárosék koncessziójának, ami a lehető legdrágább megoldás.

stories.title: Nem biztos, hogy Budapesten kell keresni Orbánék legfeleslegesebb közpénzes luxizását
stories.summary: Kultúrpalotából patrióta klubhelyiség: névleg kulturális programokat tartanának a sok milliárd forintért megvásárolt és további milliárdokból felújított brüsszeli Magyarország Házában. Csakhogy saját bevallásuk szerint idén ilyen nem volt benne júniusig, viszont a Fidesz európai pártcsaládja rendszeresen használta az épületet.

stories.title: Milliárdokat költött az állam futballstadionokra – a nézők meg öt év után otthon maradtak
stories.summary: Magyarország 2010 óta több mint 700 milliárd forintot költött futballstadionok építésére és felújítására – szinte kizárólag közpénzből. Az NB I. és NB II. mérkőzéseinek adatait elemezve azt találjuk, hogy az új stadionok megnyitása után valóban megnő a nézőszám, de ez a lendület nagyjából öt szezon után elillan. A kapacitáskihasználtság – vagyis hogy a helyek hány százaléka telik meg – tartósan nem javul. Ha az állam fizet, a klubok nincsenek arra ösztönözve, hogy megtöltsék a stadionokat.

stories.title: Körülzárt a kanadai erdőtűz egy tehervonatot, a dolgozókat úgy kellett kimenteni
stories.summary: A tartományban több mint 100 helyen ég az erdő és a bozót, ami több északnyugati települést veszélyeztet, és elérheti az Egyesült Államokat is, ahol vasárnap a foci-vb döntőjét rendezik.

stories.title: Merkely Béla nem szólt, hogy tulajdonos volt a fia cégében, amikor az az általa vezetett egyetemmel közösen százmilliós tételben nyert közpénzt
stories.summary: A KinaviX projektje Hankó Balázs egyedi döntésével kapott háromszor is támogatást, hol a Semmelweis, hol a cég neve alatt, hol közösen. A rektor az egyetemi alapítvány kuratóriumi tagja, így már tavaly jeleznie kellett volna az összeférhetetlenséget, mikor az egyeteme a céggel szerződött. Már kiszállt a KinaviXból, de részesedését a Semmelweis cége vette meg – amelynek szintén ő az egyik vezetője.

stories.title: Gajdos László: Egyszerűen nem találjuk az előző kormány 40 milliárdos velencei-tavi mentési tervét
stories.summary: Az élő környezetért felelős miniszter a Magyar Hangnak azt mondta: mostantól nem lehet majd semmilyen beruházást végrehajtani úgy, hogy a környezetvédelmi szempontokat nem veszik figyelembe.

stories.title: Lesérült a forgatáson a God of War sorozat főhőse, új színészt keresnek a helyére
stories.summary: Újra kell forgatni az eddigi jeleneteket is.

stories.title: Jeszenszky Géza: Szijjártó Péter elképesztően hiú ember, aki körülvette magát fullajtárokkal
stories.summary: A volt külügyminiszter szerint a BYD-hez igazolt Szijjártónak nincs visszaútja a magyar politikába.

stories.title: Több rendőrt és szociális munkást irányít a kormány Budapest belső kerületeibe a közbiztonság javítására
stories.summary: A polgármesterek szerint az utcákon egyre súlyosabb szociális, mentális egészséggel kapcsolatos problémák láthatók. Bevezetik a szerződéses közösségi rendőr intézményét is, mondta Magyar Péter.

stories.title: Orbán János Dénes visszavásárolná a műveit a KMTG-től, amiket 412 millióért vettek meg tőle, miközben ő szakmai vezető volt ott
stories.summary: „A közérdek mellett nekem a legnagyobb érdekem, hogy tisztázzam ezt az ügyet és a becsületemet” – írta egy nyílt levélben.

stories.title: A Gogol Bordello azért nem jött a Szigetre, mert a frontember attól tartott, elviheti Magyarországról az orosz titkosszolgálat
stories.summary: Gerendai Károly szerint más előadót is meg kellett korábban nyugtatniuk, hogy jöhetnek a fesztiválra. A tulajdonos szerint egyelőre épp a legolcsóbb diákbérlet fogy a legrosszabbul a fesztiválra.

stories.title: „Itt vannak titkos dokumentumok” – egyelőre ennyit tudni arról, hogy vizsgálják Szijjártó orosz kapcsolatait
stories.summary: Magyar Péter most először erősítette meg, hogy vizsgálat indult a volt külügyminiszter ügyében. Szijjártó Péter korábban azt mondta: még a feltételezés is sértő, hogy őt vagy Orbán Viktort fogják valamivel az oroszok.

stories.title: Az első találkozásnál Tarantino rosszul ejtette a nevét, de azért behívta a házába
stories.summary: A katonaság rakta rendbe, dolgozott cipőboltosként is, de végül színész lett belőle. Már lassan hetven éve. Interjú Harvey Keitellel.

stories.title: Magyar kutatók találtak egy kapcsolót, ami gátolhatja a tumorok növekedését
stories.summary: Az ELTE Biológiai Intézet és a HUN-REN Szegedi Biológiai Kutatóközpont együttműködésében végezték a kutatást.

stories.title: Átfogó rendészeti intézkedéseket ígér a BRFK, miközben több mint kétezer fős rendőrhiány van Budapesten
stories.summary: A rendőrség szerint a következő egy hónapban komplex közterületi ellenőrzések lesznek a főváros több pontján.

stories.title: Nem ritkák, de mindig nagy feltűnést keltenek a Szijjártóéhoz hasonló katapultálások
stories.summary: Osztrák, német, brit, brazil, ausztrál és amerikai exminiszterek és volt kormányfők is döntöttek már úgy, hogy politikai kapcsolataikat és lobbierejüket óriáscégek szolgálatába állítják. Szijjártó és a BYD esete mégsem állítható teljesen párhuzamba a legtöbb külföldi előképpel.

stories.title: Se köpni, se nyelni nem tudtunk, mondta az Erste vezére Orbán Viktor bankellenes kijelentéseiről
stories.summary: Amikor Orbán a háború kutyáinak nevezte az Erstét és a Shellt, azzal nyugtatták az európai partnereiket, hogy ez csak hungarikum, „ilyen választások előtti viccelődés”.

stories.title: Most realistic ancient roman faces revealed at Budapest exhibition
stories.summary: Sixteen faces from Roman Aquincum have been brought back to life in a new exhibition, where DNA analysis and forensic anthropology reveal the diverse backgrounds of the ancient city's inhabitants.

stories.title: As Le Pen looms, Macron and Merz bid to show Franco-German engine still has clout
stories.summary: BERLIN — Friedrich Merz and Emmanuel Macron opened two days of Franco-German talks Thursday with a pledge to strengthen Europe’s military, industrial and technological power, seeking to display that the continent’s key relationship can still deliver despite recent setbacks. The German chancellor and French president will lead defense talks at Nörvenich air base Friday before joining their ministers near Cologne. The gathering will be Macron’s final Franco-German ministerial council before he lea

stories.title: Google mega fine to slash taxpayers’ contributions to Brussels
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — U.S. tech giant Google will inadvertently help fund the EU’s coffers as the European Commission plans to put its multibillion-euro fine toward the bloc’s central budget. The €4.6 billion sanction issued to Google in 2018, which is the EU’s largest-ever single fine, will go some way toward filling countries’ spending needs for 2026, a Commission official confirmed to POLITICO. The EU budget, which is made up predominantly of government contributions, is used to finance the

stories.title: Marines board tanker amid blockade of Iranian ports as US expands strikes with attacks on bridges
stories.summary: Boarding of M/T Wen Yao in Gulf of Oman comes as expanded airstrike campaign hits five bridges in southern Iran American forces boarded a ship in the Gulf of Oman on Thursday as part of the renewed blockade of Iran’s ports that began earlier this week, the US military said. US Marines boarded the M/T Wen Yao “to ensure full compliance with the ongoing US naval blockade,” US Central Command (Centcom) said in a post on X. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany news: Macron and Merz lead bilateral cabinet talks
stories.summary: Berlin and Paris are seeking to deepen security cooperation as ministers meet in western Germany. Meanwhile, a court is set to deliver its verdict on an alleged supporter of the far-right NSU terror group. DW has more.

stories.title: Iran reports fresh strikes near Qeshm Island and accuses US over ‘barbaric’ hospital attack – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog has been paused The Lebanese foreign minister, Youssef Raggi, said Lebanon has made a decision to “end Hezbollah’s military presence” and that decisions on war and foreign policy are the “exclusive prerogative of the Lebanese state”. The Lebanese government is pushing to disarm Hezbollah, one of the most heavily armed militias in the Middle East, and it has become a central component of the US-brokered talks between Lebanon and Israel. Lebanon has made its choice: there will be no

stories.title: Political crisis and protests in Ukraine as Zelenskyy defends sacking defence minister
stories.summary: President says he had to choose ‘one side or the other’ after breakdown of relations between ministry and military leaders Ukraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy, has defended his decision to dismiss the country’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov , and confirmed reports that relations had broken down between the ministry and the country’s top army leadership. Speaking at a press conference in Kyiv with the British prime minister, Keir Starmer, Zelenskyy said there had been a “challen

stories.title: Trump rails against election systems — and familiar enemies
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Thursday announced his administration is declassifying documents that he says outline vulnerabilities in U.S. election systems. In his 25-minute speech from the White House, Trump described efforts by China to access U.S. voter rolls and offered details about long-studied vulnerabilities in electronic voting machines. But none of the information Trump described appears to support his long-running claims that the 2020 election was stolen or

stories.title: US strikes Iran for 6th day in a row
stories.summary: U.S. forces carried out a sixth consecutive day of strikes against Iran on Thursday, continuing to cast doubt on the negotiations between Washington and Tehran. The new strikes took place at 2 p.m.,  according to U.S. Central Command , “to further degrade Iranian military capabilities.” The ongoing strikes come as the U.S. reimposes a blockade of Iranian ships in the Strait of Hormuz — and as the White House continues to emphasize a peace deal remains possible. During a press briefing earli

stories.title: US TV networks split on broadcasting Trump’s election-focused speech
stories.summary: NBC and CNN stayed away from White House speech, while Fox News, MS Now, CBS and some ABC affiliates aired it The US’s largest television stations split on whether to air Donald Trump ’s White House address live on Thursday night, which was heavy on unproven accusations about the integrity of the 2020 election won by Joe Biden. While CNN, ABC and NBC chose not to air the speech live, CBS, Fox News and MS Now (formerly MSNBC) aired at least large portions of the speech live. ABC did not air the s

stories.title: How Trump’s address was bluntly aimed at destabilizing the US electoral system
stories.summary: US president using office and intelligence agencies to try to undermine confidence in elections before midterms Donald Trump used the imprimatur of the presidency and United States intelligence agencies to try to undermine confidence in American elections in a presidential address on Thursday that seemed bluntly aimed at laying the groundwork for further destabilizing the electoral system before November’s midterm elections. In his address from the East Room at the White House, Trump attempted t

stories.title: EU border chaos feared at Dover crossing as busiest summer weekend looms
stories.summary: British domestic holidays are being pushed to their highest levels since Covid The start of the peak summer season is set to bring millions of drivers on to British roads, with concerns of traffic chaos as the port of Dover faces its biggest test yet of new EU border controls. The semi-functioning entry-exit system (EES) is credited, along with the heatwaves and fears about flights after the war in Iran , with helping push British domestic holidays to its highest levels since Covid halted intern

stories.title: Burnham to say his government will be ‘unashamedly Labour’ in first speech as party leader – UK politics live
stories.summary: ‘Special conference’ to take place at noon where Burnham will be formally announced as Labour leader Zack Polanski , the Green party leader, used to be relatively positive about the prospect of Andy Burnham becoming Labour leader. More recently he has sounded less enthusiastic, particularly after the reports saying Burnham is going to appoint Shabana Mahmood, the home secretary, as his chancellor. In an interview on Sky News this morning, Polanski said he was worried that Burnham could end up li

stories.title: US strikes on Iran reportedly hit airport and bridges – Middle East crisis live
stories.summary: Iranshahr airport, southern rail station and two bridges attacked, Iranian media says The Iranian health ministry said 38 people have been killed in Iran in recent US attacks, with more than 400 others injured. Reporting the figures on X, Hossein Kermanpour, head of public relations for Iran’s ministry of health, said 22 women and nine children were among the injured. Continue reading...

stories.title: Outrage at sacking of Ukraine’s defence minister overshadows Starmer’s final visit as PM – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed. Read the rest of our Europe coverage here in Madrid Meanwhile, the EU’s top court has ruled that a controversial Spanish law that offered an amnesty to those who planned and participated in the failed and illegal push for Catalan independence does not violate the bloc’s rules. Continue reading...

stories.title: Iran war: Tehran warns of wider disruption amid US strikes
stories.summary: The US launched a sixth night of strikes on Iran. Tehran has reportedly told Yemen's Houthis to be ready to disrupt Red Sea shipping if Iranian power infrastructure is targeted. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Xi Jinping calls for international cooperation in AI development at WAIC in Shanghai
stories.summary: Xi Jinping marked his first in-person attendance at the World ​Artificial Intelligence Conference (WAIC) in Shanghai, where he shared China's vision of AI diplomacy to challenge US dominance in the sector.

stories.title: India news: Modi silent as Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike enters 20th day
stories.summary: As Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike entered its 20th day and the "Cockroach" party continued its protest, Prime Minister Narendra Modi faced criticism for his reluctance to hold press conferences. DW has more.

stories.title: Mandela Day: Has South Africa failed his vision?
stories.summary: Three decades after apartheid, many South Africans say Nelson Mandela's vision remains unfulfilled. Inequality, xenophobia and anti-migrant protests continue to test his legacy.

stories.title: Ukraine lawmakers approve Koretskyi as new PM amid protests
stories.summary: It is President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's second Cabinet shakeup in a year. He faces criticism for dismissing reformist Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

stories.title: US slaps 25% tariffs on Brazil with election looming
stories.summary: The US will impose a 25% tariff on most imports from Brazil starting later this month. It's the first action in the Trump administration's new tariff strategy, after the Supreme Court struck down its prior impositions.

stories.title: EU court endorses Spanish PM's contentious Catalan amnesty
stories.summary: The EU's top court has backed a Spanish law giving amnesty to Catalan separatists over the independence referendum. The deal let Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez stay in office in 2023 and prompted major opposition protests.

stories.title: Germany massively expands federal police powers
stories.summary: The German government has expanded the powers of its federal police, especially in the use of surveillance, AI, and drone defenses. But experts warn of constitutional challenges.

stories.title: Trump revives claims of election interference during address to nation
stories.summary: The US president said he would declassify intelligence data that revealed China had illicitly acquired 220 million US voter files. Trump's claim contradicts reviews that have found no evidence of significant interference in the 2020 election.

stories.title: Trump alleges 'shocking vulnerabilities' in US election security ahead of midterms
stories.summary: The president accuses China of meddling in the 2020 election, directly contradicting past findings from US intelligence.

stories.title: US strikes Iranian civilian infrastructure, Tehran retaliates with attacks on US facilities in Gulf
stories.summary: In a primetime address to the nation, Trump said the US was 'winning big in Iran' and the American public would see 'the fruits of that labour'. However, a deal seems far out of reach as fighting in the region continues to escalate.

stories.title: US launches new strikes as Iran says civilian infrastructure hit
stories.summary: Tehran says the US strikes hit bridges, while the US boards a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Kyiv hit again as Ukraine reports two dead in Russian strike
stories.summary: Russian missiles killed two people and wounded five, including a child, in Kyiv, Ukraine, on 16 July 2026.

stories.title: US strikes first tanker with missiles as it enforces new blockade
stories.summary: The US military targeted an unladen oil tanker which it said was heading for the key Iranian oil terminal on Kharg island.

stories.title: Kyiv protesters rally after Zelenskyy sacks defence minister Fedorov
stories.summary: Hundreds protest in Kyiv after President Zelenskyy dismisses Defence Minister Fedorov, a moderniser credited with slowing Russia’s advance.

stories.title: Zelensky's removal of popular defence minister sparks protests in Ukraine
stories.summary: A rift between Fedorov and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi was apparently at the heart of Zelensky's decision.

stories.title: Andy Burnham’s new (and old) look No. 10 takes shape
stories.summary: LONDON — With just days to go before he becomes prime minister, Andy Burnham is still scratching his head over how to assemble his Cabinet — but his No.10 team is taking shape.  Seasoned operatives will enter Downing Street with the soon-to-be Labour leader Monday — joining familiar faces from the Keir Starmer’s administration. As Burnham’s chief of staff, James Purnell will be the beating heart of the operation. The Tony Blair-era minister left Westminster for high-flying roles as a

stories.title: Andy Burnham set to become UK Labour leader — live updates
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will become the next leader of Britain’s governing party Friday, before replacing Keir Starmer as British prime minister early next week. At a special conference in London, the Makerfield MP — who returned to parliament last month after nine years as Greater Manchester mayor — will be elected unopposed as Labour’s next leader. No other Labour MP received the required nominations after an overwhelming majority of the parliamentary party endorsed Burnham’s candidacy, which comes

stories.title: Moroccan intelligence insider reveals widespread use of Pegasus hacking software
stories.summary: Whistleblower suggests internal security services deployed spyware from 2017 against key domestic and foreign targets A former member of Morocco’s domestic intelligence service has helped to provide an unprecedented insight into how the north African state used hacking software – including Pegasus spyware – to target journalists, human rights defenders, French politicians and Spanish cabinet ministers and police officers. Pegasus, which is manufactured by the Israel-based NSO Group, allows its o

stories.title: Family devastated by ‘unacceptable’ charges over methanol deaths of Australian teenagers in Laos
stories.summary: Australian government says it is ‘deeply frustrated’ and will continue to press for ‘real charges with teeth’ over deaths of Bianca Jones and Holly Morton-Bowles Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government says it wants “real charges with teeth” over the fatal methanol poisoning of two teenage travellers in Laos, as authorities prepare to lay lesser charges that have been called “bitterly” disappoin

stories.title: Neglected software update caused widespread Telstra network outage, CEO tells Senate inquiry
stories.summary: Vicki Brady told senators maintenance staff were unaware of mass outage risk as critical software failure ‘rippled slowly across the network’ Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Telstra has blamed the lack of a software update on a key time-keeping system for an outage that caused nationwide chaos last week, with its maintenance teams also unaware of a design change that affected how it would reset. stories.title: Pauline Hanson laments end of White Australia policy and talks up daughter’s leadership potential
stories.summary: Appearing on a podcast with the far-right UK activist who goes by the name Tommy Robinson, Hanson makes evidence-free claims about migrants Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Pauline Hanson has used an interview with the far-right British activist Tommy Robinson to blame the end of the White Australia policy for creating what she sees as problems with migration and said her daughter, Lee Hanson, was the future of On

stories.title: Sinkhole on Sunset Boulevard: broken water pipe floods LA-area hotspot
stories.summary: Businesses and traffic in West Hollywood doused after century-old pipe bursts belowground A century-old water pipe buried below a bustling boulevard in a Los Angeles county city burst early Thursday, sending a shock of water spewing into the streets and snarling traffic as several major roads shut down. The 36in riveted steel pipe’s rupture left a protruding sinkhole and cracked pavement in West Hollywood. Images of the aftermath showed local metro buses partially submerged in murky brown water.

stories.title: Trump rehashes wide-ranging US election claims and releases heavily redacted documents in primetime address – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. stories.title: Cyclist Rohan Dennis pleads guilty to driving with suspended licence after crash that killed wife
stories.summary: Adelaide-based Olympian was banned from driving for five years over Melissa Hoskins’ 2023 death Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Former professional cyclist Rohan Dennis faces a jail sentence after admitting he drove while disqualified over the events leading up to the death of his Olympian wife, Melissa Hoskins. A judge had previously warned the former Olympian he would be jailed if he drove while banned. Continu

stories.title: No deposit, no problem: the new 100% mortgages for first-time buyers
stories.summary: Banks and building societies have started relaxing affordability rules and becoming more creative with products For many first-time buyers, getting their foot on the property ladder can feel like an impossible dream. However, the good news is that there are a growing number of mortgage deals that require only a small deposit, or no deposit at all. Metro Bank is the latest high street lender to launch a deal that allows eligible first-timers to borrow up to 100% of the value of a property. H

stories.title: ‘How’s this joker got my details?’: BrewDog founder faces complaints over emails to ‘equity punks’
stories.summary: Exclusive: Watchdog asked to look into how James Watt got data of ex-crowdfunders he invited to join buy-back bid James Watt, the BrewDog founder who sold the debt-laden “punk” brewer earlier this year , is the subject of complaints to the UK’s data privacy watchdog linked to his surprise bid to buy the company back, the Guardian has learned. BrewDog’s brand, intellectual property, UK breweries and 11 bars were sold to the US cannabis and drinks firm Tilray in March for £33m, in a deal that rend

stories.title: Andy Burnham’s difficult first cabinet – a visual guide to the options and dilemmas
stories.summary: The Makerfield MP enters No 10 as prime minister on Monday. But who will he choose to join him at the table? Keir Starmer is expected formally to resign as prime minister on Monday morning at Buckingham Palace, with Andy Burnham being invited to take on the role shortly afterwards. Once back in Downing Street mid-morning, Burnham will begin assembling his cabinet. Continue reading...

stories.title: China ‘strongly dissatisfied’ with nationalisation of British Steel
stories.summary: Move dealt ‘severe blow to Chinese companies’ confidence in investing in the UK’, says Ministry of Commerce China’s government has said it is “strongly dissatisfied” with the decision to nationalise British Steel this week, 15 months after the UK government stepped in to prevent the closure of its steelworks in Scunthorpe and the loss of 4,000 jobs. On Thursday, British Steel was brought under public ownership to protect “the future of steel production”, the government announced. Continue readin

stories.title: Trump makes unverified claims of China ‘election meddling’ as critics fear ploy to challenge midterm results
stories.summary: Opponents warn president’s address about 2020 election loss is attempt to sow confusion ahead of midterms that could deliver big losses for Republicans Donald Trump accused China of interfering with the 2020 election in a primetime televised address that laid bare his continuing obsession with his defeat to Joe Biden, but which opponents warned was a smokescreen for him to meddle in the forthcoming congressional midterms. In a 25-minute speech on Thursday that had been hyped by Trump himself, th

stories.title: Trump’s Board of Peace drops full Gaza recovery plan in favour of tiny pilot scheme
stories.summary: Revised plan aims to ‘keep something going’ amid fears Netanyahu may gamble on new all-out offensive before Israeli elections The Gaza recovery plan being pursued by Donald Trump’s Board of Peace (BoP) has shrunk dramatically from an ambitious blueprint for the reconstruction of the whole territory to a small pilot project in the south of the strip. Even the envisaged pilot scheme – involving a temporary camp for a tiny fraction of Gaza’s 2 million displaced people, with a Palestinian administra

stories.title: Target of Monaco bomb blames attack on Ukrainian spy agency
stories.summary: Vadym Iermolaiev says military intelligence officers were behind assassination attempt that seriously wounded partner and child • Monaco bomb mystery deepens as Ukraine’s security services linked to murder of prime suspect The Ukrainian businessman who survived an assassination attempt in Monaco has accused Ukraine’s military intelligence agency of organising the attack. In his first public statement since the bombing that shook the wealthy principality, Vadym Iermolaiev alleged that serving and

stories.title: Mafia law gives Italian families right to break free from life of crime
stories.summary: Wives and children offered new identities to try to stop gangsters recruiting down the generations Children and young adults raised in mafia families will be given a chance to break away from organised crime under new legislation in Italy that aims to stop the intergenerational recruitment of gangsters. In an unprecedented effort to sever the family chain, the Italian state will offer children aged under 25 and other close relatives of mafia bosses a chance to start over: a new home in another c

stories.title: Former boss of Italian motorways sentenced to 12 years over Genoa bridge tragedy
stories.summary: Giovanni Castellucci among 32 people convicted over the 2018 Morandi Bridge collapse, which killed 43 Thirty-two people, including the former chief executive of Italy’s motorway operator, have been convicted over the 2018 collapse of a Genoa bridge in which 43 people died. In a hushed courtroom on Thursday in the north-western Italian city, Giovanni Castellucci, a former boss of Autostrade per l’Italia, was sentenced to 12 years in prison, the highest in the case. Continue reading...

stories.title: Donald Trump accuses China of meddling in US elections
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has again claimed he has evidence of interference in the 2020 presidential election, this time alleging China obtained 220 million voter records. China rejects the claim.

stories.title: AI: Why Europe is falling behind, and how it can catch up
stories.summary: Europe trails the US and China in artificial intelligence. Economist and Nobel laureate Philippe Aghion says better research funding, venture capital and AI innovation can help it catch up.

stories.title: Monaco assassination plot takes a new turn
stories.summary: A woman suspected of plotting to assassinate a Ukrainian-Cypriot businessman in Monaco has been found dead in Ukraine. Two men with ties to Ukraine's intelligence services have now been brought into custody.

stories.title: Italy: Ex-highway head sentenced to 12 years for deadly bridge collapse
stories.summary: The collapse of the Morandi bridge in Genoa in 2018 killed 43. The court found the former Italian highway CEO guilty of neglecting maintenance despite prior warnings and sentenced him to 12 years in prison.

stories.title: Iran war: US military attacks Iran for sixth night running
stories.summary: US Central Command said that a new wave of strikes was launched on Iran. It follows retaliatory strikes from Iran on sites in Jordan and Gulf states.

stories.title: Kris Jenner's mother Mary Jo dies aged 91

stories.title: Pele's 1958 World Cup final shirt sells for $4.9m

stories.title: Spain battles major Aragon wildfire amid heatwave
stories.summary: Spain has deployed more than 400 firefighters and 19 aircraft to combat a fast-moving wildfire in the northeastern region of Aragon after flames swept across around 7,600 hectares by 16 July.

stories.title: JD Vance accuses Israel of 'manipulating' US public opinion to prolong Iran war
stories.summary: The US vice president says he thinks some Israeli politicians are trying to detail efforts to end the conflict.

stories.title: As heatwaves strike, Europeans turn to Chinese air-conditioners
stories.summary: Chinese brands are meeting surging air-conditioner demand in Europe, a region with typically low ownership rates.

stories.title: He escaped the Bangkok bar inferno - but his girlfriend and three bandmates didn't
stories.summary: Athipat Wichan tells BBC Thai he regrets bringing his girlfriend Nahathai into his indie band Thotsakan.

stories.title: At least one dead in Texas floods ravaging same area where campers died
stories.summary: The same area devastated by floods last year's floods, which caused more than 130 deaths, is once again being hit.

stories.title: Rubik’s Cube stars shine at European speedcubing event in the Netherlands
stories.summary: More than 1,000 speedcubers from 53 countries are competing in Arnhem, the Netherlands, from 16 to 19 July 2026. Events range from blindfolded solves to solving while wearing oven gloves.

stories.title: One man's hunger strike changed India's map. Can it still change politics?

stories.summary: Smoke from more than 800 blazes has filled major cities from Toronto and New York, to parts of the US Midwest and Great Lakes.

stories.title: Philippines condemns monkey video on Chinese media as racist
stories.summary: The AI video on China Daily showed a monkey in Philippine garments singing its claims to the South China Sea.

stories.title: Australia 'deeply frustrated' over Laos methanol poisoning charges
stories.summary: Media reports say Laos will press charges of up to one year in jail against those allegedly responsible.

stories.title: More than 500 Rohingya vanished at sea - what happened?
stories.summary: Two boats carrying an estimated 530 Rohingyas have disappeared since leaving Myanmar on 29 June.

stories.title: Olympic cyclist admits to driving despite ban after crash that killed wife
stories.summary: Rohan Dennis pleaded guilty to driving while disqualified after police pulled him over last month.

stories.title: Japan relaxes royal succession rules - but ban on female emperors remains
stories.summary: The law now allows the adoption of male distant relatives aged over 15 back into the imperial family.

stories.title: School bus crash kills at least 20 pupils in Uganda
stories.summary: Dozens others, including school staff, were injured in the crash, suspected to have been caused by a mechanical fault.

stories.title: Japan revises imperial succession law but retains ban on female emperors
stories.summary: Nationwide opinion polls in Japan have suggested broad public support for a female emperor, but some conservative political figures remain opposed to the idea.

stories.title: Trump Media to sell instant access to 'market-moving' social posts
stories.summary: Trump Media is launching a fast, paid feed to its most influential posts for Wall Street traders.

stories.title: China hits out at British Steel nationalisation
stories.summary: The UK government said taking the firm into public hands would safeguard "a vital national capability".

stories.title: Tehran vows to 'crush' regional infrastructure if US hits Iranian sites
stories.summary: Iran said its response to any US strike on its infrastructure would result in destruction across the region, as multiple reports said Tehran had asked the Houthis to be ready to close the Bab el-Mandeb Strait, threatening a second global shipping chokepoint.

stories.title: Supporters welcome France's assisted dying law
stories.summary: Supporters welcomed France's assisted dying law after parliament approved the landmark legislation, calling it "a huge relief" for people with incurable illnesses. The law still requires approval from France's Constitutional Council.

stories.title: Watch: What to know as Genoa bridge disaster trial ends
stories.summary: More than 50 defendants have been on trial in Italy over the 2018 bridge collapse which killed 43 people.

stories.title: Trump teleprompter operator suspended for alleged prediction market bets on speeches
stories.summary: US media had reported that Gabriel Perez, who has been operating Trump’s teleprompter since 2016, used his inside knowledge to win more than $100,000 (€85,900) betting on what the president would say in big speeches.

stories.title: Mexico asks US state attorneys general to investigate immigrant ICE deaths
stories.summary: Since start of Trump’s second term, 14 Mexican immigrants have died in ICE custody and three in agency operations Mexico formally requested that US state attorneys general criminally investigate cases of immigrants who have died in Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) custody or during raids, the Mexican government said Tuesday. The request follows the death of Mexican immigrant Lorenzo Salgado Araujo , who was shot and killed by an ICE agent in Houston. Since the beginning of Donald Trump

stories.title: US resumes daylight strikes on Iran as tensions escalate over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US Central Command said it has resumed daylight strikes on Iran, targeting coastal defences and missile sites. Tehran has reported casualties as tensions rise over a renewed blockade threat, calls for retaliation and the ongoing dispute over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran launches retaliatory attacks on US bases in Kuwait, Bahrain and Jordan

stories.summary: Banner refers to Falkland Islands conflict in 1982 UK business secretary calls for Fifa investigation The Argentina players celebrated their World Cup win over England with a banner saying “ Las Malvinas son Argentinas ”, making reference to the 1982 Falklands war . stories.title: Uber to buy Germany’s Delivery Hero in $14.8bn global deal
stories.summary: Agreement combines Uber Eats with food delivery brands in 99 countries and expands taxi service reach Business live – latest updates Uber has reached an agreement to take over the German takeaway company Delivery Hero in a $14.8bn (£11bn, or €12.9bn) deal that would create a global food delivery giant. The US tech firm said it had offered to pay €41.50 a share to Delivery Hero’s shareholders, valuing the business at $14.8bn. Uber will pay $13.7bn after accounting for its previous purchases of a

stories.title: Revealed: how Europe’s most powerful farming lobby killed EU’s pesticide law
stories.summary: Exclusive: High-level documents show how Copa Cogeca worked to weaken legislation to protect climate and wildlife Europe live – latest updates Newly revealed documents from inside the most powerful farming lobby in Europe show how it delayed, gutted and overturned some of the most sweeping farming reforms in EU history, including a plan to cut pesticide use in half. Copa Cogeca describes itself as the voice of 22 million farmers across the continent, and enjoys unrivalled access to EU lawmakers.

stories.title: Uganda calls for travel restrictions to be lifted after last Ebola patient discharged
stories.summary: Country begins 42-day countdown to outbreak being declared officially over, as numbers continue to rise in neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo Uganda has started lobbying countries to lift Ebola-related travel restrictions after discharging its last confirmed Ebola patient from hospital. The discharge of a Congolese national from the Mulago national referral hospital’s isolation centre in Kampala on Thursday triggered the start of a 42-day countdown required by the World Health Organiz

stories.title: ‘We weren’t at fault’: British yacht couple bristle at ‘armchair sailors’ and Russian denials
stories.summary: Exclusive: Jane and Alan Kelvey reflect on close encounter with Russian warship a few hours into two-month sailing trip They found themselves at the centre of an international incident, the close encounter between their small sailing boat and a Russian warship making headlines around the world . A month later, Jane and Alan Kelvey are to be found berthed in a rainy harbour in north-west France, still taken aback by their brush with Vladimir Putin’s forces – but trying to get on with their fun sa

stories.title: UK aid cuts ‘reduce bilateral support to some African countries by 90%’
stories.summary: Critics say Foreign Office figures send ‘global message about the role the country wants to play on international stage’ Labour’s foreign aid cuts mean reductions of as much as 90% in the bilateral support the UK will give to some African countries, Foreign Office figures show. The department’s annual report includes a long-awaited breakdown of how the reduction in the aid budget will affect individual countries for the next three years. Continue reading...

stories.title: US intensifies attacks on Iran as Tehran hits back at Gulf states
stories.summary: Sixth day of fighting threatens to turn into all-out war and casts serious doubt on peace deal struck last month Chaos and confusion bring US no closer to resolution on strait of Hormuz The US has intensified its attacks on Iran, hitting targets near Tehran and striking a ship it accused of trying to break its blockade, while Iran retaliated by firing missiles and drones at US allies in the region. Six consecutive days of back-and-forth attacks threaten to pull the region back into a total war a

stories.title: Will gold prices extend their record-breaking run?
stories.summary: Predictions suggest the price of the precious metal will keep hitting new record highs as investors seek a safe bet in turbulent times. Some forecasts say prices could double within five years.

stories.title: The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?
stories.summary: The emissions trading system became a global blueprint for pricing carbon and cutting CO2. Now industry groups are pushing back against tougher climate rules. How effective is the scheme and where does it fall short?

stories.title: Germany may make it illegal to deny Israel's right to exist
stories.summary: Germany's upper house of parliament, the Bundesrat, has introduced a bill seeking to ban the questioning of Israel's right to exist. It is now up to the lower house to decide whether to turn it into law.

stories.title: Algeria: Fire at orphanage kills 11, including children
stories.summary: A fire at an orphanage in the province of Algiers has killed 11 people and injured 19 more, authorities said. President Abdelmadjid Tebboune expressed his condolences amid his trip to Berlin.

stories.title: As the world rearms, South Korea aims to cash in
stories.summary: Weapons and military equipment produced in South Korea are sought-after due to their reliability, speedy delivery times and relatively low prices. Global demand for weapons has surged after Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

stories.title: Why America's hospitals keep looking to India for nurses

stories.title: Fact check: Claims linking statins to dementia are false
stories.summary: Viral posts claim that a drug widely used to lower cholesterol is contributing to a dementia epidemic. DW Fact Check examined the scientific evidence.

stories.title: Why Gulf states are investing billions in Africa
stories.summary: Wealthy Gulf states are investing billions in African countries. But the nations involved all have different objectives. What's behind the investments and what's in it for African countries?

stories.title: German tenants need better heat protection
stories.summary: Heat waves are becoming longer, hotter and more frequent. Record temperatures have left many tenants struggling. Now Chancellor Friedrich Merz has said the building law needs to be amended.

stories.title: "It's freedom": Border residents react to end of Gibraltar-Spain checks
stories.summary: Routine border checks between Spain and Gibraltar have ended, allowing thousands of daily commuters to cross more easily. While many welcome shorter journeys and greater freedom, others remain cautious about the long-term impact.

stories.title: Canada: train crew escapes flames near Armstrong, Ontario
stories.summary: A train crew was surrounded by wildfire flames near Armstrong, Ontario, Canada, on 13 July 2026. The Canadian National Railway crew escaped safely as more than 160 fires burned across the region.

stories.title: Fraud crackdown on Ghana ID cards makes digital checks compulsory - photocopies banned
stories.summary: Companies are now required to verify a person's identity electronically to curb theft and forgery.

stories.title: China is 'a critical long-term strategic challenge', EU ministers say
stories.summary: EU foreign ministers issued a joint assessment of the risks posed by China, warning that Beijing threatens not only the bloc’s economy but also its security, while highlighting the shared ambition of China and Russia to assert regional dominance.

stories.title: Volunteer firefighter suspected of starting devastating France forest fire
stories.summary: President Macron said there would be no leniency for arsonists, as France - along with much of Europe - deals with a series of unusual heatwaves.

stories.title: Watch: What we know about newly discovered monkey species

stories.title: Children among dead at Algeria foster care home blaze - president
stories.summary: Eleven people have died and 19 other have suffered injuries in the overnight fire, the authorities say.

stories.title: Authorities deny reports of booms in downtown Dubai

stories.title: 'Moment of joy' as Uganda discharges last Ebola patient

stories.summary: Algeria is probing a deadly orphanage fire near Algiers, early on 16 July, that killed at least 11, injured 19 and struck amid a heatwave and nearly 1,000 recent blazes.

stories.title: Italian officials handed jail terms for Genoa bridge disaster that killed 43
stories.summary: The ex-head of Italy's motorway operator Giovanni Castellucci was handed a 12-year term over the 2018 bridge collapse.

stories.title: Fire at Algerian orphanage kills 11 children, injures 19
stories.summary: Algerian authorities did not say what caused the deadly blaze, which occured amid a heatwave that has sparked nearly 1,000 fires.

stories.title: White House teleprompter operator accused of making $100k from Trump speech bets
stories.summary: A White House staffer has been accused of using inside knowledge of speeches to make nearly $100,000 on Kalshi.

stories.title: Baj van a részeg, vérengző tengerésszel
stories.summary: Pontosabban nincs, mert az Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced legalább olyan jó, mint az eredeti játék, megtartva annak minden erősségét. Kis grafikus ráncfelvarrás itt, némi extra tartalom ott, és a rajongóknak sincs okuk panaszra.

stories.title: Kettős győzelemmel jutott a Fradi az Európa-liga második fordulójába
stories.summary: A nyolcadik perctől emberelőnyben játszó kupagyőztes hazai pályán 3-0-ra verte a szerb Vojvodinát.

stories.title: Ezekért érdemes moziba menni július 17-tól július 24-ig
stories.summary: Itt a következő hét napra szóló Telex Diszpó.

stories.title: Donald Trump közösségi médiája információs előnyt adna annak, aki fizet ezért

stories.summary: Az egyedi gyűrűből 2026 darabot készítenek, ebből harmincat kapnak a győztes csapat tagjai.

stories.summary: A hétfőn elfogadott jogszabály szerint visszahívhatóvá válna a Kúria és a bírósági hivatal vezetője is, de annak módja sok aknát rejt.

stories.summary: A kínai gyártók megsokszorozták az európai eladásaikat, de így sem jutott mindenkinek berendezés.

stories.title: China’s economy grows at 4.3%, one of its lowest rates on record
stories.summary: Worse-than-expected figures for three months to June come amid concerns over lopsided economy Business live – latest updates China has posted worse-than-expected growth figures for the three months to June as its economy expanded by just 4.3% – one of its lowest quarterly readings on record. The rate, which came in under the government’s target of 4.5% to 5%, was one of the weakest since reporting on official quarterly GDP figures began in the early 1990s. Continue reading...

The agreement, which was signed in Brus

stories.title: Five arrested in Hong Kong bookstore raids in ‘seditious’ materials crackdown
stories.summary: Third round of arrests linked to independent bookshops widely regarded as clampdown on dissent in territory Hong Kong authorities have raided two bookstores and arrested five people on suspicion of selling allegedly seditious publications, in the latest step targeting independent booksellers. Videos and photos from multiple media outlets on Wednesday showed officers wearing vests marked with “police” seizing boxes from the building that houses Have A Nice Stay, a bookshop founded by former journ

stories.title: Fears for New Zealand’s native species as first bird flu case emerges
stories.summary: Minister urges public to report cases of three or more sick or dead birds in a group after brown skua seabird tests positive for H5N1 on Wellington beach The deadly H5N1 bird flu has been confirmed in New Zealand for the first time, sparking alarm that some of the country’s most beloved and vulnerable native birds could be wiped out if it spreads. stories.title: Brazil vows to retaliate if US imposes 25% tariffs on some of its products

stories.title: More than 500 feared dead after reports of two shipwrecks off Myanmar, UN says
stories.summary: Vessels believed to have departed from Myanmar in late June, with mostly Muslim Rohingya minority onboard The United Nations has said more than 500 people are feared dead after reports of two large shipwrecks off Myanmar since late June. stories.title: No support for Jews fleeing Nazi Germany at Evian conference
stories.summary: In July 1938, a conference was held in Evian, France, to address the issue of Jews being persecuted by the Nazis in Germany and Austria. Almost nobody in the world was willing to take them in as refugees.

stories.title: East-West border death zone now Germany's biodiversity oasis
stories.summary: Many endangered species now live in the area that once formed the border between East and West Germany. Environment Minister Carsten Schneider recently visited the "Green Belt," and DW went along for the ride.

stories.title: ICE should continue traffic stops after shootings, Trump says, apparently contradicting new policy

stories.title: Iran launches strikes on neighbours as Trump threatens to hit power plants

stories.title: Singapore court orders Bloomberg to pay ministers $356,000 in defamation case
stories.summary: Bloomberg’s editor-in-chief, John Micklethwait, stands by reporting, saying ministers who sued ‘imposed an extremely strained meaning on what was a solid ​story’ Bloomberg News and one of its reporters have been ordered to pay S$460,000 (US$355,734) in damages after an article it published was found to have defamed two Singapore government ministers, the city-state’s high court said in a judgment released on Tuesday. Bloomberg and the reporter, Low De Wei, are liable to jointly pay S$230,000 to

stories.title: US strikes Bushehr as Iran's parliament tables bill to control Hormuz
stories.summary: Four points in the port city hosting Iran's only nuclear power plant were hit by US projectiles on Tuesday, as Iran's parliament formally submitted a bill asserting Tehran's permanent control over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Surviving extreme heat increasingly boils down to this: access to air conditioning | Mark Wolfe
stories.summary: The next great climate divide will be between countries that have the resources to adapt and those that don’t This summer, much of the media’s attention has focused on record temperatures across Europe and the United States. Television coverage has been filled with familiar images: heat maps shaded deep red, schools closing, rail lines slowing, wildfires spreading and emergency rooms treating growing numbers of people with heat-related illnesses. Public officials have responded with equally fami

stories.title: Wildfires in Ontario make Toronto air quality worst in world
stories.summary: Environment Canada has issued health warnings after sky over city turns yellow Smoke from more than 100 active wildfires in northern Ontario have made Toronto’s air quality the current worst in the world and caused yellow, smoky air in cities across the north-east US. Environment Canada issued health warnings on Wednesday after the sky over country’s largest city turned a sickly yellow and was ranked the worst in the world according to IQAir , the Swiss technology company that racks global air q

stories.title: Woman speaks out on husband being pulled from Ryanair plane – video
stories.summary: Svetlana Grković and her husband, Ljubisa Karović, had just settled into a Ryanair flight last week when a loud bang pierced the hum of engines. Within seconds, she saw her 61-year-old husband being sucked out of the plane through a dislodged cabin window. Karovic is now being treated in hospital in Thessaloniki with severe neck and arm injuries, Maksimovic said ‘If we die, we die together’: wife of Ryanair passenger almost sucked through window speaks Continue reading...

stories.title: How global heating supercharged floods in West Africa, displacing thousands
stories.summary: Adaptation to frightening new normal and reducing emissions further and faster is critical, scientists warn Dozens of people drowned, hundreds had to be rescued and thousands were displaced when floods struck the coasts of west Africa last month. Now scientists have concluded that the rains that caused the floods were supercharged by climate breakdown. Global heating, they say, turned what should have been a routine weather event into a climate catastrophe. Continue reading...

stories.title: Letartóztatásba került a kábítószer-kereskedelemmel gyanúsított pécsi önkormányzati képviselő
stories.summary: A politikust 2–8 évig terjedő szabadságvesztéssel büntetendő bűncselekmény elkövetésével gyanúsítják.

stories.title: Valaki végrehajtót küldött Balásy Gyula cégére
stories.summary: Pedig információink szerint a cég számláinak zárolásán enyhítettek, a zárolás elrendelése óta befolyt pénzekhez állítólag már hozzáfér a csoport.

stories.title: Feljelentést tettek a Gondosóra program miatt
stories.summary: Az Integritás Hatóság köztes jelentést készített a program egyes beszerzéseiről, a feltárt körülmények alapján több bűncselekmény gyanúja miatt feljelentést tett.

stories.title: Ez teljesen lehoz az életről: 15 éves vagyok, és még soha nem láttam Olaszországot játszani a vb-n
stories.summary: Olaszország utoljára 2014-ben jutott ki a foci-vb-re, azóta három alkalommal is elbuktak a selejtezőkön. Ki a felelős ezért? Milyen út vezetett a gödör mélyére? Van-e jövője az Azzurriknak? Olaszokat kérdeztünk a csapatuk hiányáról, és a foci számára elveszett fiatal generációról.

stories.title: Zelenszkij a védelmi minisztérium és a hadsereg vezetése közötti párbeszéd hiányával indokolta Fedorov leváltását
stories.summary: Az ukrán elnök szerint a felek nélküle nem ültek le egymással tárgyalni.

stories.title: Lüktető hipnózis, ég és föld között
stories.summary: A Karakter programajánlója csütörtöktől csütörtökig.

stories.title: Mi történik a használt ruhánkkal, miután a Mohu utcai konténereibe dobjuk?
stories.summary: Géprongy lehet egykori kedvenc felsőnkből, de akár egy erőműben vagy nyugat-afrikai piacon is kiköthet. Ehhez azonban előbb több ember kezén át kell mennie.

stories.title: A NAV vizsgálja az ukrán pénzszállító elleni akciót, és annyit megerősített, hogy két vezetőt leváltottak
stories.summary: Egyes intézkedéseknél szabálytalanságot állapítottak meg, ezért a hivatal újabb átvilágítást rendelt el.

stories.title: Beigazolódott a gyanú, új majomfajra bukkantak Kongó erdeiben
stories.summary: A helyiek már húsz éve ismerik Likweliként, most kapott egy sokkal unalmasabb latin nevet.

stories.title: A hegyvidéki önkormányzat szerint életveszélyes az Erzsébet-kilátó
stories.summary: Azt írták, nincs pénzük a felújításra, a kormányhoz fordultak segítségért.

stories.title: A brit kormány minden jót kíván a döntősöknek, „de különösen Spanyolországnak”
stories.summary: Nem hagyták szó nélkül az argentinok molinós ünneplését.

stories.title: Vitézyék arról is megkérdezik az embereket, hogy legyen-e életkorhoz kötve az e-rollerek használata
stories.summary: A kormány közlekedésbiztonsági kérdőíve arra is kiterjed, hogy kapjanak-e szigorúbb büntetést a visszaeső gyorshajtók.

stories.title: Az emberek több mint kétharmadát elküldték a digitális polgári körök mögött álló cégből, ami azt állítja: önkéntesekkel folytatja tovább
stories.summary: Az RTL Híradója szerint a kéttojásos kampányt fizető másik cégnél a hatvan emberből csak kettő maradt.

stories.title: Why Indonesia's economy is under pressure
stories.summary: Southeast Asia's largest economy risks losing its emerging-market status as President Prabowo Subianto's spending plans rattle markets. Can he deliver growth?

stories.title: Running dry: How to store more groundwater for dry seasons
stories.summary: More frequent weather extremes are leading to water shortages during droughts and flooding during heavy rain. But groundwater storage can help secure water for dry seasons while mitigating extreme rain.

stories.title: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes appeal to AfD voters
stories.summary: Germany's chancellor has insisted that his coalition has "regained its footing" — despite dismal poll numbers. Friedrich Merz also cautioned against voting for the far-right AfD party.

stories.title: Could Russia be about to lose its grip on chess leadership?
stories.summary: Russians have long pulled the strings when it comes to the governing body of international chess. But now two Germans are vying for FIDE's top job.

stories.title: One dead and three missing after boat sinks near San Francisco's Alcatraz prison island
stories.summary: Authorities did not know if the boat was headed to Alcatraz, a major tourist attraction, where shuttered buildings of the famous former prison still stand.

stories.title: Iran executes man over January protests as crackdown on dissent widens
stories.summary: Mohammad Amini Dehaghani was convicted of setting fire to a governor's office in Isfahan on 9 January, as Amnesty International says Iran is using wartime conditions as a pretext to accelerate politically motivated executions.

stories.title: Italy: melting ice statues protest heatwave conditions in Rome
stories.summary: Greenpeace Italy and the CGIL union melted ice statues outside Rome's Colosseum on 15 July 2026. The protest highlighted the impact of heatwaves on outdoor workers and called for a fossil fuel phase-out.

stories.title: Dramatic video shows wildfire surround freight train in Canada
stories.summary: CN Rail told the BBC that operations were temporarily suspended in the affected area in the province of Ontario.

stories.title: Ukraine: Six dead as Russia targets Sumy and Odesa
stories.summary: Emergency crews battled fires and searched damaged buildings across Ukraine on Tuesday 15 July after Russian strikes on the cities of Sumy and Odesa killed six people and injured at least 20 others, according to regional authorities.

stories.title: Po river choked by algae bloom as Italy battles heat and navigability woes
stories.summary: Italy is stepping up efforts to clear thick algae from the River Po in Turin after weeks of high temperatures accelerated its spread along the country’s longest river.

stories.title: More world leaders arrive in Doha to honour Qatar's former emir
stories.summary: European delegations continued to arrive in Qatar on the third day of national mourning, with France, Germany and Britain among those sending senior representatives, as Iran's foreign minister, the UAE president, Jordan's king and Palestinian president also paid their respects.

stories.title: What if Bluey spoke one of the world's oldest living languages?
stories.summary: Five episodes of the award-winning children’s cartoon have been released in Australian Indigenous language for the first time.

stories.title: New monkey species with orange lips found 'hiding' in DR Congo forest
stories.summary: Scientists describe "amazing feeling" to look into the face of an animal that so few people knew existed.

stories.title: Orange haze from Canada wildfires seen in Ontario and northern US
stories.summary: There are currently 838 wildfires actively burning across the country, according to the Canadian Interagency Forest Fire Centre.
